_meta.scored_by: Claude Opus 4.7 (same session as scenario author; scenarios were authored before skill body was read — see independence stance in findings.md)
_meta.what_is_scored: runtime-layer invariants (core/synthesis.py + core/synthesis_policy.py + core/writeback/proposal.py). Skill-layer narration is NOT scored here — would require invoking Claude Code as a subprocess per scenario, which is out of scope for an automated eval pack.
_meta.rationale_quality_skipped: All four scenarios show 'skipped' for rationale_quality because the synthesis skill is not invoked in the runner. This is a known eval-pack limitation, recorded in findings.md.

scenarios.action_correctness_notes: All pass_criteria met. The runtime's firing-generation is structurally orphan-free — X7 iterates proposals to emit firings, so no firing can have an affected_domain not present in the proposal set. The 'orphan case' the scenario specifies is preserved by construction, not by skill handling. Both recommendations correctly capped from 'high' to 'moderate' confidence.
scenarios.rationale_quality_notes: Not testable without invoking the synthesis skill as an agent subprocess.
scenarios.uncertainty_calibration_notes: Confidence cap correctly applied: both proposals started 'high', both final recommendations 'moderate'. Cap is independent from soften/block per tier precedence — verified via persisted confidence values.
scenarios.finding: Good news: the current runtime prevents orphan firings by construction. Bad news: the scenario as written can't exercise what it was designed to exercise (skill-level orphan handling) because the runtime never emits an orphan firing. Invariant holds, but via a different mechanism than the plan's scenario assumed.

scenarios.action_correctness_notes: Phase A cap works correctly on both recovery and running proposals (confidence: high → moderate). Phase B adjust (X9) CANNOT be exercised against v1 because nutrition is not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS — no nutrition proposal can be submitted, so X9 returns empty by construction. The two-phase cap+adjust stacking invariant is therefore UNTESTABLE end-to-end against current code. Code review of guard_phase_b_mutation (core/synthesis_policy.py:651) confirms the write-surface guard structurally enforces the plan's invariants — adjust may only mutate action_detail; action mutation rejected with XRuleWriteSurfaceViolation. Partial credit reflects: cap works; adjust guard is correctly implemented per code review but not exercisable in current scope.
scenarios.uncertainty_calibration_notes: Cap correctly applied. Phase B cap stacking with adjust cannot be uncertainty-checked without Phase B firing.
scenarios.finding: Phase B machinery is intact and correctly guarded at the code level, but cannot be exercised against v1's proposal surface. Re-run this scenario as a Phase 5 gate once nutrition lands.

scenarios.action_correctness_notes: Runtime preserves deferred proposals verbatim (running's 'defer_decision_insufficient_signal' + low confidence flowed through unchanged). No spurious X-rule firings against the already-deferred proposals — confirmed by zero phase_a_firings. Recovery's 'proceed_with_planned_session' with high confidence preserved correctly. Full 4-domain mixed-missingness cannot be exercised because sleep + strength proposals are not submittable against v1; partial test of the invariant. Missingness-tag surfacing at the rationale/narration layer (pass_criterion 5) is not testable without skill invocation.
scenarios.uncertainty_calibration_notes: Running's low confidence preserved; recovery's high confidence preserved (no firings → no cap). Correct for the testable subset.
scenarios.finding: Runtime correctly respects already-deferred proposals and does not invent data. Full missingness-tag propagation at the skill layer is unverified (requires running the agent + the 4-domain surface). Re-run as a Phase 3 gate once sleep + stress are first-class.

scenarios.action_correctness_notes: Stale schema_version (0.1 instead of recovery_proposal.v1): rejected with ProposalValidationError, invariant='schema_version', clean named message listing the expected value. Out-of-enum action ('moonshot_tempo_plus_long_run'): rejected with ProposalValidationError, invariant='action_enum', message lists the full expected enum set. proposal_log row count is 0 — neither proposal landed. Synthesis attempted on the empty proposal_log raised SynthesisError, not a silent success. All 4 pass_criteria satisfied.
scenarios.uncertainty_calibration_notes: Errors are loud, invariant ids are structured, and the synthesis-layer refusal is explicit. No silent consumption of malformed input.
scenarios.finding: Input validation path is the strongest result in the eval. Named invariants + clean stderr + no silent synthesis consumption. This is the pattern other layers should aim for.

totals.gate_decision_per_rubric: 4 of 4 ≥ 2/3 → Phase 3 authorised unconditionally — BUT two of four passes are scope-gap (untestable parts of the invariant). See findings.md for the caveat.
